literal: to give up one's life as the highest form of sacrifice
a final performance or celebration before ending or retiring

relabelled_senses.definition: At the latest possible moment before a deadline or event; happening just before it is too late.
A person who spends money extravagantly or lavishly; often used as a playful or flattering label for someone noted for conspicuous consumption.
A final course of action or option adopted only when all other possibilities have failed or are exhausted.

literal: a final gathering or event marking the end or farewell

relabelled_senses.definition: The final annoyance or problem in a series that causes one to lose patience or take action.
An additional problem, complication, or undesirable item that would be wholly unwelcome in a given situation.
The final trumpet blast prophesied in Christian eschatology that signals the resurrection of the dead and the Last Judgment.
The final remark or statement in a discussion or argument; the final say that ends or settles a dispute.
A vehicle or manufactured item of recent manufacture or a recent model year; a comparatively new or modern example of its kind.
Figuratively agitated, excited, or emotionally worked up; overly upset or riled.
To make a large profit, often easily or despite criticism or questionable means; to benefit financially from a situation while others do not.
Extremely amusing; continuously funny or entertaining.
A person or thing that is the object of ridicule, mockery, or scorn.
An ineffectual or clumsy tackle in a sport that invites ridicule or laughter.
To laugh quietly or inwardly, especially at someone else's misfortune or folly; to suppress outward signs of amusement.
To laugh very loudly or uncontrollably; to find something extremely funny.
To reject or dismiss an argument, proposal, or claim as absurd or untenable, so that it will not be taken seriously.
Variant of 'laugh in one's sleeve'; to be secretly amused or pleased, often at another's expense, while concealing outward amusement.
